title: Citrus Smoky Russian Turkey Neck Casserole
description: A cozy, dairy‑free casserole that blends smoky turkey necks with bright citrus and a crunchy crumbly topping, perfect for a comforting dinner.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat 2 tbsp olive oil over medium heat. Add onions, garlic, carrots, and potatoes; sauté until onions are translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add smoked paprika, cumin, salt, pepper, and bay leaf; stir for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the broth and bring to a simmer. Reduce heat to low, cover, and let the vegetables cook for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
While the vegetables are cooking, prepare the citrus mixture: combine lemon zest, lemon juice, orange zest, orange juice, and chopped dill in a small bowl.
Add the smoked turkey neck pieces to the skillet, pour in the citrus mixture, and stir gently to combine. Simmer uncovered for another 10 minutes, allowing flavors to meld.
In a separate bowl, mix rye breadcrumbs, almond flour, 1 tbsp olive oil, and a pinch of salt to form a crumbly crust.
Transfer the casserole to a greased baking dish, top evenly with the crumb mixture.
Bake in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the crust is golden and the casserole is heated through.
Remove from oven, let rest 5 minutes, then serve warm.
Enjoy your citrusy, smoky, dairy‑free Russian comfort dish!
